message: fix: skip empty likes in index/fts

files.patch:
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 {"id":"xf-9rf.5","title":"Fresh-eyes bug hunt + correctness fixes","description":"Goal:
- Conduct a careful, methodical bug hunt across the codebase and fix issues with minimal, test‑backed diffs.

Steps:
1) Re‑read critical paths (parser/search/storage/CLI) with fresh eyes.
2) Identify correctness risks: unchecked assumptions, lossy conversions, silent fallbacks, bad error handling.
3) For each issue:
   - Document root cause, impact, and reproduction steps.
   - Add a failing unit/integration test **before** the fix when feasible.
4) Apply minimal fix using `apply_patch`; no unrelated refactors.

Testing & Verification:
- Add unit tests for each fix; add or extend E2E scripts when behavior changes are user‑visible.
- E2E scripts must include detailed logs (command, stdout/stderr, exit code, timing).
- Run `cargo check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant tests.

Acceptance:
- All fixes include tests and root‑cause notes.
- No regressions; quality gates recorded.
- Any behavioral changes documented with before/after examples.
","notes":"Fresh-eyes fixes: enforce manifest.js presence (parse_manifest uses required reader + test), enable prefix matching by indexing with default tokenizer and querying text_prefix, add prefix search test, and update cli_e2e fixtures with manifest + JSON validation logging. +{"id":"xf-cfn","title":"Skip empty like text in search index/FTS","description":"Likes with empty full_text were still indexed/FTS-inserted, causing indexable_document_count mismatch and possible empty results. Skip empty text consistently in SearchEngine::index_likes, store_likes FTS insert, and FTS rebuild/missing checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:15:20.601465596-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T15:16:04.524496976-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T15:16:04.524496976-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"xf-d3b","title":"Fail fast on unknown embedding doc_type when writing vector index","description":"write_vector_index currently maps unknown doc_type to tweet (code 0). That silently mislabels embeddings and violates metadata correctness. Validate doc_type and embedding length; fail with clear error.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T14:31:43.652625554-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T14:39:25.119971143-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T14:39:25.119971143-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"xf-g5v","title":"Replace nonexistent optimize/reindex suggestions with real commands","description":"Doctor/index health checks suggest 'xf optimize' or 'xf reindex', which are not real commands. Update suggestions to existing commands (e.g., 'xf index --force' or 'xf doctor --fix').","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T14:52:26.041209055-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T14:54:46.707897071-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T14:54:46.707897071-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"xf-mox","title":"Fix get_by_ids underfetch when untyped lookups collide with multiple doc types","description":"get_by_ids uses TopDocs limit = lookups.len(); when lookups contain untyped ids, multiple doc types can match and crowd out other ids. Raise search limit based on typed vs untyped lookups, and add regression test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T14:44:35.51679244-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T14:46:22.333568695-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T14:46:22.333568695-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
